approvedBy: Steve hard-checkpoint priority + Codex Foundation builder session
approvalSource: Steve captured the Foundation-up capability registry requirement so provider/tool capabilities like Fal, ElevenLabs, Canva, and terminal workers are registered before agent use, with explicit no hidden subagents and no side-effect default.
scopeSummary: Define and prove the Foundation-up provider/tool capability registry for Fal, ElevenLabs, Canva, and terminal workers; record env refs, permission, cost, audit logs, callable path, owner, proof, and approval boundaries while keeping runtime/provider/agent use blocked.
notApproved: Do not call Fal, ElevenLabs, Canva write/export/upload/design APIs, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or other providers.
Do not spend provider credits or run paid/model/media generation.
Do not launch terminal workers, hidden subagents, invisible workers, or parallel builders.
Do not grant Harlan, Crewbert, role assistants, or extraction workers new runtime authority.
Do not mutate Drive, Gmail, ClickUp, Slack, Agent Feedback, Canva, or external systems.
Do not run live extraction, source crawls, transcript fetches, screenshots/keyframes, downloads, summarization, vision, or model calls.
Do not store or print secret values.
